message:
fix(agent_config): report accurate file_exists in error plan items

When `build_agent_configure_error_plan_item` constructed a plan item for
a failed configuration step, it hard-coded `file_exists: false` even
when the target file was actually present on disk (e.g. unreadable due
to permissions).  This caused dry-run output to misleadingly claim the
file did not exist.

Add `fallback_agent_config_file_exists()` which resolves the target path
and checks `path.exists()`, mirroring the approach already used by
`fallback_agent_config_filename()`.  The error plan builder now calls
this helper instead of returning a literal `false`.

Also clarify the doc comment on `AgentConfigurePlanItem::section_exists`
(and its JSON schema counterpart) to note that `false` may indicate
"unknown" when planning fails before the file can be inspected.

Includes a Unix-only test that creates an unreadable target file,
triggers a planning error, and asserts the resulting plan item has
`file_exists: true`.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
